ranked_frameworks.name: Cascading Vacancy Allocation Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: In systems where agents occupy discrete, size-ranked positions and cannot share positions, a single new resource at the top generates a deterministic reallocation cascade whose total welfare gain far exceeds the value of the initial resource alone. System-wide mobility is controlled not by individual ambition but by the rate of top-tier vacancy creation.

ranked_frameworks.name: Metallurgical Paradigm Shifts
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: The history of metallurgy follows a Kuhnian structure: long periods of normal metallurgy (incremental alloy refinement within an established paradigm of microstructural control) punctuated by revolutionary paradigm replacements. The bronze-to-iron transition, the Bessemer process, and the emergence of high-entropy alloys each represent crises where accumulated anomalies — alloys that outperformed theoretical predictions or failed inexplicably — forced a reconceptualization of what governs material properties. The current multi-principal-element alloy revolution is a paradigm shift in real time, replacing the base-metal-plus-dopants framework with a configurational entropy paradigm.

ranked_frameworks.name: Memetic Formulaics
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Internet memes propagate via oral-formulaic composition — viral content succeeds not through novelty but through recombination of prefabricated structural formulas (templates, formats, cadences) that reduce cognitive load on both producer and consumer, exactly as Homeric epithets and ring composition enabled oral epic transmission.

ranked_frameworks.name: Judgment Sensitivity Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Human judgments decompose into discriminability (d') and criterion bias, and aggregation protocols improve accuracy primarily by increasing collective d-prime rather than canceling bias — structured protocols narrow the noise distribution itself, while averaging exploits independent noise.

ranked_frameworks.name: Allostatic Load Theory of Software Degradation
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Software systems accumulate 'allostatic load' through repeated adaptive responses to stressors (deadline patches, scope changes, dependency churn). When cumulative wear exceeds repair capacity, the system enters allostatic overload — a phase transition where failures cascade and incremental fixes accelerate degradation.

ranked_frameworks.name: Attentional Divergence Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Any adversarial system operates by engineering a growing gap between the target's mental model and actual state, using calibrated attention sinks to consume cognitive resources on visible decoys while the operative payload executes below the monitoring threshold. The attack succeeds when the target's model-of-reality and reality itself diverge past a critical threshold.

ranked_frameworks.name: Crystallization-Preservation Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: All complex adaptive systems containing a fluid substrate and a structurable solute undergo spontaneous crystallization — the irreversible-seeming but reversible transition from fluid dynamism to rigid structure — as a natural preservation mechanism. The rate is governed by a universal trio: saturation ratio, ambient energy, and nucleation seeds. Reversal requires controlled reintroduction of energy without destroying the preserved content.

ranked_frameworks.name: Vitreous Development Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: All creative output exists on a cooling-rate spectrum between amorphous glass (maximum sharpness, minimum durability) and crystalline rock (maximum durability, minimum edge). The optimal strategy is to anneal: ship glassy prototypes to find the sharpest edge, then selectively reheat and slow-cool only the fragments that cut deepest.

ranked_frameworks.name: Interface Matrix Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Productive complexity emerges at boundaries between two distinct systems when structural elements extend into the transitional zone and form a three-dimensional network performing three universal functions: accretion (trapping mobile resources), incubation (sheltering vulnerable new entities), and dampening (absorbing systemic shocks from either side).

ranked_frameworks.name: Bio-Social Oscillator Unification
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Firefly synchronization and crowd synchrony are instances of a single universal coupling mechanism: phase-locked oscillators with threshold-triggered pulse coupling. Only three parameters govern emergence — coupling strength, refractory period, and neighbor detection radius. Any population of discrete oscillators meeting these constraints will synchronize on a timescale proportional to log(N).

ranked_frameworks.name: Nutrient-Desert Predation Architecture
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: In any resource-scarce ecosystem, organisms and institutions converge on a universal predation architecture: attract via mimicked abundance, capture via irreversible commitment mechanism, and extract through slow digestion. The two-trigger verification system is an energy-economics optimization distinguishing environments where predation is viable from those where passive gathering suffices.

ranked_frameworks.name: Stigmergic Supply Networks
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Supply chains can be restructured as decentralized, pheromone-like signal networks where each transaction deposits a 'cost pheromone' on the route it traverses. Routes with lower total cost accumulate stronger signals through faster capital turnover, attracting more traffic, while market volatility acts as evaporation to prevent lock-in. No central planner is needed; the network self-optimizes toward cost-minimal, resilient multi-source routing.

ranked_frameworks.name: Symbiotic Resilience Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Complex adaptive systems recovering from acute stress follow a universal bifurcation pattern: recovery quality depends not on returning to the prior state but on whether the system can recruit new mutualistic components before opportunistic dominants lock in a degraded regime. The recovery window is temporally narrow and environmentally gated, meaning the conditions surrounding recovery matter more than the severity of the initial shock.

ranked_frameworks.name: Frustule Ossification Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: All protective structural frameworks exhibit monotonic constraint accumulation: each iterative reproduction inherits the tighter of its parent dimensions, progressively narrowing the permissible action space until a costly 'auxospore event' becomes existentially necessary. The geometry of the protective structure acts as a selective filter that determines not just what threats are excluded but what innovation can permeate.

ranked_frameworks.name: Cognitive Brine Pool Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Organizational knowledge silos behave as cognitive brine pools: specialized expertise accumulates in departmental depressions until it reaches a critical density that resists dilution into the broader organization. Innovation emerges not within the silo nor in the general organization, but at the chemocline-like interface where specialists and generalists exchange partial understanding, producing hybrid insights neither group could generate alone.

ranked_frameworks.name: Quench Fracture Theory of Resilience
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Any system that gains strength through rapid surface stabilization over an unresolved interior operates in a 'quench fracture' regime: it trades gradual failure modes for catastrophic ones. The strength-to-brittleness ratio is fixed by the cooling rate; faster hardening produces higher apparent strength but guarantees total fragmentation at the failure threshold. True toughness requires annealing — slow, controlled re-heating that allows interior tension to equilibrate with the surface.

ranked_frameworks.name: Dormant Capital Germination Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: All resilient systems maintain an invisible reservoir of latent capacities whose diversity necessarily exceeds the active system's visible expression. Recovery from disruption is governed not by new acquisition but by differential reactivation sequences, where generalist elements activate first to reshape conditions that then trigger specialist emergence. System resilience is a function of dormant bank diversity and disturbance-to-activation signal matching.

ranked_frameworks.name: Controlled Discharge Architecture
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Any system under accumulating tension will discharge inevitably; the only design variable is whether that discharge follows a pre-engineered low-resistance path or fractures through the structure unpredictably. For any domain where pressure builds continuously and cannot be prevented, the optimal intervention is not suppression but the installation of a preferential discharge point that converts destructive release into structured signal.

ranked_frameworks.name: Adaptive Precedent Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: All complex adaptive systems that must respond to recurring challenges develop a dual-speed architecture: slow deliberative first-response followed by rapid pattern-matched recall, where the initial encounter permanently biases the recognition template. This creates a fundamental tradeoff between response speed and accuracy-to-novelty, as the system's 'memory' increasingly diverges from the evolving challenge landscape through 'founding fixation.'

ranked_frameworks.name: Herd Immunity as Nash Equilibrium Collapse
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: The herd immunity threshold can be reframed as a Nash equilibrium problem: each individual's vaccination decision is a strategy in a population game where payoffs depend on others' choices. Below the critical immunization fraction, the 'free-ride on herd immunity' strategy is not yet available, so vaccination dominates. Above it, free-riding becomes rational, creating a game-theoretic trap where the population oscillates around the threshold. Applying Nash equilibrium analysis with heterogeneous risk preferences predicts the precise vaccination coverage shortfall better than classical SIR models that assume homogeneous rational agents.

ranked_frameworks.name: Textile Phase Transition Dynamics
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Woven fabrics undergo a phase-transition-like behavior at critical thread densities: below a threshold cover factor, the fabric behaves as a loose collection of independent threads with negligible emergent properties; above it, inter-thread friction and geometric locking produce a sudden onset of collective mechanical behavior — shear resistance, drape, and dimensional stability. This transition is governed by the same mathematical structure as percolation thresholds in statistical physics, and predicting it requires knowing the coupling strength (friction coefficient) and the lattice geometry (weave pattern) rather than the properties of individual threads.

ranked_frameworks.name: Edaphic Trophic Cascade
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Introducing or removing top predators in the soil food web (predatory nematodes, microarthropods) triggers cascading effects on soil structure itself — not just on organism populations. Predator removal causes bacterial or fungal blooms that alter aggregate stability, pore architecture, and water retention, propagating effects downward through trophic levels to reshape the physical soil matrix. Soil management should therefore treat predator guilds as keystone regulators of soil structure, not merely of nutrient cycling.

ranked_frameworks.name: Red Queen Jurisprudence
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Legal systems and the actors they regulate are locked in a co-evolutionary arms race: new regulations select for novel compliance-evasion strategies, which in turn select for more sophisticated regulatory language. Like host-parasite genotype cycling, the most common regulatory framework is the one most heavily exploited, creating frequency-dependent selection where legal innovation must run continuously just to maintain the same level of enforcement effectiveness. Regulatory stasis is not stability — it is the moment before the parasitic strategy overtakes the host.

ranked_frameworks.name: Bayesian Soil Contamination Inference
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Soil remediation decision-making can be formalized as Bayesian updating over a hypothesis space of contamination plume geometries. Each new borehole sample serves as evidence that updates the posterior probability distribution over plume extent, concentration gradients, and migration direction. The optimal sampling strategy is the one that maximizes expected information gain per borehole dollar, and remediation should commence not when contamination is confirmed but when the posterior probability of exceeding regulatory thresholds crosses a decision-theoretic cost boundary.

ranked_frameworks.name: Costly Signal Gating Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Systems facing high false-positive costs evolve multi-threshold activation gates where each successive trigger narrows the probability space, and the energy cost of the response mechanism itself enforces the requirement for redundant confirmation. The gate count converges on the minimum number where false-positive cost times false-positive rate falls below the per-gate maintenance cost.

ranked_frameworks.name: Superspreader Topology for Architectural Ventilation Design
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Epidemiological superspreader topology theory shows that heavy-tailed contact distributions produce outsized transmission from a few high-contact nodes. Applying this to building ventilation: airflow networks have their own 'superspreader' zones -- spaces where recirculated air from many zones converges before redistribution. These HVAC superspreader nodes amplify contaminant transmission disproportionately relative to their volume. Designing ventilation networks with superspreader topology analysis -- identifying and filtering at high-centrality air-mixing nodes rather than uniformly across all zones -- achieves equivalent pathogen reduction at 40-60% lower energy cost compared to uniform filtration strategies.

ranked_frameworks.name: Morphogen Gradient Dyeing Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Gradient dyeing techniques in textile engineering can be formalized using the morphogen gradient framework from developmental biology. The dye source acts as a morphogen-producing organizer, the fabric as the tissue field, and dye concentration thresholds determine color-zone boundaries with the same mathematics as Wolpert's positional information model. Predicting and controlling gradient dye patterns therefore reduces to managing source concentration, diffusion coefficients in the fiber matrix, and threshold sensitivities of different dye-fiber binding reactions — a framework that unifies resist-dyeing, dip-dyeing, and injection printing under one quantitative model.

ranked_frameworks.name: Alloy Satisficing
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Metallurgical alloy design under real industrial constraints operates not by optimization but by satisficing: engineers search the composition-processing space and accept the first alloy meeting a threshold bundle of properties (yield strength, corrosion resistance, weldability, cost). The search order — starting from known alloy families and perturbing — determines which local satisfactory solution is found, producing path-dependent alloy traditions that vary by industrial culture. This explains why different national metallurgical traditions converge on different standard alloys for equivalent applications rather than discovering a single optimum.

ranked_frameworks.name: Impedance-Matched Pedagogy
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Acoustic impedance matching — where energy transmission across a boundary is maximized when the impedances of the two media match — provides a quantitative framework for instructional design. The teacher and student each have a 'cognitive impedance' determined by their knowledge density, processing speed, and representational format. Maximum knowledge transfer occurs when these impedances are matched. Scaffolding functions as an impedance transformer (like the bones of the middle ear), converting high-impedance expert knowledge into a form that matches the student's lower impedance. The Zone of Proximal Development is precisely the impedance-matching bandwidth — the range of frequencies (concept complexities) that can be transmitted with acceptable loss.

ranked_frameworks.name: Reaction-Diffusion Patterning of Urban Segregation
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Residential segregation patterns arise from a Turing-type instability in the coupled reaction-diffusion of two social groups. When the 'activation' rate of same-group clustering exceeds the 'inhibition' provided by cross-group economic interdependence, and when social diffusion rates differ between groups, stable spatial patterns of segregation spontaneously emerge from initially well-mixed conditions. The characteristic wavelength of neighborhood size is set by the ratio of social mobility rates between groups — predicting that unequal mobility produces finer-grained patchwork.

ranked_frameworks.name: Cryptographic Trophic Cascade
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: In layered cryptographic ecosystems, the compromise of a top-level primitive (e.g., a widely-used hash function) propagates downward through the dependency stack in a manner structurally identical to a trophic cascade. Removing the apex predator — the hardness assumption that keeps attackers in check — releases suppressed attack vectors at every lower level, causing protocol-level 'population explosions' of vulnerabilities. The magnitude of the cascade is governed by the topology of the dependency web: monoculture cryptographic stacks experience devastating cascades, while diverse stacks exhibit damped, localized effects.

ranked_frameworks.name: Soil as Autopoietic System
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Mature soil is an autopoietic system: its biological and mineral components continuously regenerate the organizational structure that maintains the soil body's identity. Root exudates feed microbes that produce binding agents that stabilize aggregates that create pore space that sustains root growth. When this self-producing loop is disrupted (tillage, sterilization), the soil loses organizational closure and degrades toward an inert mineral substrate. This reframes soil health not as a checklist of chemical parameters but as the integrity of a self-maintaining boundary between organized pedosphere and disordered parent material.

ranked_frameworks.name: Taphonomic Bias Theory of Historical Memory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Collective memory of historical events is governed by preservation biases identical in structure to fossil taphonomy: only events embedded in favorable 'sedimentary' conditions (institutional recording, material culture, emotional salience) survive into the historical record, and the processes of decay, compression, and mineralization systematically distort what persists. The historical record is not a sample of the past but a taphonomic residue shaped by the chemistry of cultural preservation. Historiographic correction requires identifying the specific 'diagenetic' processes — political rewriting, archival neglect, medium decay — that have sculpted what appears to be evidence.

ranked_frameworks.name: Stimulated Emission Rhetoric
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Laser physics' stimulated emission — where a photon passing an excited atom triggers release of an identical, phase-coherent photon, producing exponential amplification of a single mode — models how rhetorical cascade events work. In a population with high 'rhetorical inversion' (many people holding strong but unexpressed opinions), a single well-crafted public statement (seed photon) can trigger coherent, synchronized expression of identical sentiments. The 'optical cavity' is the media ecosystem that reflects and re-amplifies the message. The result is not merely viral spread but phase-coherent amplification: everyone saying the same thing at the same time with the same emotional valence, producing a rhetorical laser beam of extraordinary persuasive intensity.

ranked_frameworks.name: Liminality as Psychosocial Phase Transition
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Rites of passage operate as controlled phase transitions in social identity: the liminal phase is the critical point at which the old identity structure loses long-range order before the new one crystallizes. Turner's communitas is the analogue of critical fluctuations — temporary equality and mutual recognition that exists only at the transition temperature. Societies that eliminate structured liminal experiences force individuals through uncontrolled phase transitions, producing the social equivalent of amorphous glass rather than well-ordered crystal: functional but brittle identities.

ranked_frameworks.name: Preferential Attachment Corrosion Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Pitting corrosion on metal surfaces follows preferential attachment dynamics: existing pits that are already large attract disproportionately more corrosive ions due to their deeper electrochemical gradients, growing faster than smaller pits in a rich-get-richer feedback loop. The resulting pit size distribution follows a power law, and the largest pit at any time can be predicted from the total pit population and the attachment exponent. Protective coating strategies should therefore focus on preventing the first few pits from reaching critical depth rather than uniformly reducing corrosion rate, because once preferential attachment takes hold, the distribution becomes dominated by extremes.

ranked_frameworks.name: Taphonomic Selection Theory of Historical Memory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Collective memory is not shaped primarily by the importance of events but by taphonomic selection — the differential preservation of evidence based on material and institutional 'sediment chemistry.' Events that occurred in contexts with robust recording media, institutional archivists, and stable political strata fossilize into the historical record; equally significant events in volatile or orally-mediated contexts decompose without trace. The historical canon is therefore a biased fossil assemblage, not a representative sample, and its apparent patterns reflect preservation filters as much as actual event distributions.

ranked_frameworks.name: Synaptic Niche Construction
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Active synapses modify their local biochemical microenvironment (the 'synaptic niche'), which reshapes selection pressures on future plasticity. Learning is co-evolution between neural activity and the micro-environment it constructs.

ranked_frameworks.name: Decision Tempo Nudging
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Choice architecture can be optimized by matching nudge timing to the OODA decision cycle — interventions placed at the Orient phase (when mental models update) are disproportionately effective compared to those at Decide or Act, because they reshape the framing before options crystallize.

ranked_frameworks.name: Preferential Pattern Accretion
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Design patterns accumulate connections proportional to existing connectivity, causing a few hub patterns to dominate built environments while most remain peripheral — producing scale-free topology rather than Alexander's egalitarian web.

ranked_frameworks.name: Nested Loop Habit Architecture
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Sustainable behavior change requires engineering three nested habit loops — micro (seconds), meso (session), macro (identity) — where each loop's reward serves as the cue for the next iteration. Keystone habits succeed when they function as the core loop of a personal behavioral game.

ranked_frameworks.name: Titled Memory Governance
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Rust's ownership model maps to property law: every memory value has exactly one owner (title holder), borrowing follows lease semantics, and lifetimes are easement durations. This reframes memory safety as a legal governance problem with enforceable property rights.

ranked_frameworks.name: Pellicle Theory of Organizational Culture
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Team culture forms like a kombucha SCOBY: a symbiotic colony of practices and values that creates a protective pellicle at the interface between the team and its environment. The pellicle is both product and producer of the culture beneath it.

ranked_frameworks.name: Derelict Systems Salvage Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Abandoned complex systems follow identical legal and operational dynamics governing claim, custody, and value preservation. The tension between law of finds (full ownership) and law of salvage (compensated stewardship) maps onto the rewrite-vs-refactor decision, where institutional memory constitutes archaeological value.

ranked_frameworks.name: Single-Stroke Intervention Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Optimal intervention in any complex adaptive system requires a single, precisely calibrated action along the system's natural grain rather than repeated or broad-spectrum forcing. The interventionist must read the substrate to identify the correct angle, force, and timing that preserves systemic integrity.

ranked_frameworks.name: Necrophoretic Organizational Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Organizations operate a continuous death-signal detection system where units must actively suppress markers of failure through performative vitality signaling. Units that fail to mask decline markers trigger automated removal processes regardless of actual productivity, creating systematic vulnerability to signal-based misclassification.

ranked_frameworks.name: Macrophagic Persistence Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Institutional memory persists not because organizations actively maintain it but because certain knowledge is continuously consumed and re-expressed by institutional macrophages — individuals and processes that ingest departing knowledge and re-present it to new members, creating the illusion of permanence through constant turnover.

ranked_frameworks.name: Complication Load Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Complexity cost per module grows super-linearly because each new module must negotiate constraint surfaces with all existing modules, not just the base platform. A system's maximum viable complication count is determined by the ratio of base-architecture tolerance to inter-module coupling density, and exceeding this threshold produces catastrophic rather than graceful degradation.

ranked_frameworks.name: Checkpoint Hijack Theory of Institutional Immunity
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Complex adaptive systems maintain integrity through inhibitory checkpoints that distinguish self from threat. Parasitic agents survive by co-opting checkpoint signaling to mask exploitation as legitimate function. Restoring defense requires checkpoint disruption, which carries an inherent tradeoff: the same removal of brakes that enables attack on parasites also risks autoimmune destruction of healthy components.

ranked_frameworks.name: Interstitial Assembly Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: The capability of any composite system is governed more by the quality of its negative space — the interfaces, gaps, and relational channels between units — than by the quality of the units themselves. Optimizing unit-to-unit fit requires irreversible reshaping, and emergent function is only legible at a scale removed from individual components.

ranked_frameworks.name: Civilizational Subduction Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Cultural collisions operate through a subduction mechanic: the less institutionally dense culture is forced beneath the dominant one, where its practices are heated under social pressure until they partially melt and re-emerge as hybrid expressions — syncretic religions, creole languages, fusion cuisines. The collision zone generates social friction, new institutional formations, and deep identity crises whose intensity correlates with the density differential.

ranked_frameworks.name: Cryptobiotic Organizational Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Organizations facing existential stress should execute a deliberate, reversible metabolic shutdown — replacing liquid operational capacity with stabilized minimal structures. Survival duration scales not with reserves burned during active resistance, but with the fidelity of the preservation state and the speed of rehydration protocols pre-encoded before shutdown.

ranked_frameworks.name: Symbiotic Entity Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: High-performing entities in hostile environments are obligate symbioses between a structural partner (resource acquisition, protection) and a generative partner (core value creation). Resilience is proportional to the tightness of metabolic coupling, and failure mode is almost always decoupling rather than external destruction.

ranked_frameworks.name: Institutional Petrification Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Institutions undergo petrification: a molecule-by-molecule replacement of original purpose, personnel, and animating culture with bureaucratic mineral — procedural compliance, credentialism, self-perpetuation — while preserving the exact external structure of the founding organization. The result is an entity that exhibits perfect morphological fidelity to its origin while being composed of entirely different operational substance.

ranked_frameworks.name: Latent Succession Infrastructure Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Complex systems that persist across leadership transitions maintain a dormant developmental pipeline — organisms or candidates kept in an undifferentiated state, activatable by environmental signals. The cost of maintaining idle succession capacity is justified because the alternative produces colony or organizational collapse at a rate that exceeds the maintenance cost over evolutionary or market timescales.

ranked_frameworks.name: Pod Strategy Lock-In Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Coordinated teams develop a single dominant strategy through cultural transmission and reinforcement, creating deep competence but structural rigidity. Switching strategies carries prohibitive retraining costs because the coordination patterns, role specializations, and tacit knowledge are co-adapted to one approach. Optimal performance requires accepting strategic monomorphism rather than pursuing versatility.

ranked_frameworks.name: Caldera Governance Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Organizational crises that fully exhaust a central authority's resources trigger an inward structural collapse analogous to caldera formation, where the resulting vacuum is not mere absence but a topologically distinct basin that preferentially accumulates novel talent, projects, and culture impossible under the prior regime. The depth and completeness of collapse determines ecosystem richness, suggesting organizations should resist premature re-centralization.

ranked_frameworks.name: Attention Allocation Games in Neurolinguistic Processing
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Game theory's attention allocation framework -- where players distribute finite cognitive attention across simultaneous strategic interactions -- directly models how the brain allocates processing resources across concurrent linguistic tasks (reading while listening, simultaneous translation, monitoring multiple conversations). The model predicts that linguistic tasks with higher 'payoff volatility' (ambiguous syntax, unfamiliar accents) command disproportionate attention, creating neglect-induced errors in concurrent lower-volatility tasks. This explains why simultaneous interpreters make more lexical errors during syntactically complex passages: attention is reallocated to the higher-variance syntactic game.

ranked_frameworks.name: Marine Biofilm Stigmergy
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Sessile marine organisms colonizing hard substrates (coral larvae, barnacles, tube worms) practice a form of stigmergy: early colonizers deposit biochemical traces that alter the substrate's chemical and textural properties, indirectly guiding the settlement decisions of later arrivals. The resulting community structure is not centrally coordinated but emerges from layered environmental modification, producing predictable succession sequences. Reef restoration could be dramatically improved by engineering artificial substrates that replicate the stigmergic trace signatures of mature reef biofilms rather than the structural geometry alone.

ranked_frameworks.name: Rhetorical Impedance Matching
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Persuasive communication fails at audience boundaries for the same reason acoustic energy reflects at medium interfaces: impedance mismatch. A speaker's rhetorical register (vocabulary density, emotional amplitude, epistemic assumptions) functions as communicative impedance. When it closely matches the audience's cognitive-cultural impedance, argumentative energy transmits efficiently. Mismatched impedance causes reflection — the audience hears but does not absorb. Skilled rhetoricians intuitively perform impedance matching by modulating register, just as horn loudspeakers use flared geometry to match air impedance.

ranked_frameworks.name: Covenantal Versioning
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Covenant theology's mechanism — where successive covenants in divine history supersede prior ones while fulfilling their essential promises — maps precisely onto software API versioning strategy. Each API version constitutes a 'covenant' between provider and consumer. A new version must fulfill the core commitments of the prior covenant (backward-compatible data contracts) while superseding its operational terms (deprecated endpoints, new authentication). The 'redemptive completeness' metric becomes API coverage completeness: a version history is well-designed when each successor makes the prior covenant unnecessary while honoring every promise it made.

ranked_frameworks.name: Crystal Nucleation Theory of Social Movements
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Social movements form through a nucleation mechanism: diffuse grievances exist in a supersaturated solution of discontent, but collective action only crystallizes when a critical nucleus of committed actors exceeds a threshold size. Below this critical radius, movements dissolve back into atomized complaint. Above it, crystallization propagates rapidly through the population. Impurities — pre-existing organizational structures, charismatic leaders — lower the nucleation barrier, explaining why movements often catalyze around existing institutions rather than forming de novo.

ranked_frameworks.name: Quantum Circuit Compilation of Fermentation Pathways
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Metabolic pathway optimization in industrial fermentation operates under the same pressure as quantum circuit compilation: the goal is to minimize the total number of enzymatic steps (gates) and the longest sequential dependency chain (circuit depth) to reach a target metabolite, subject to the 'native gate set' of available enzymes and the 'hardware connectivity' of metabolic network topology. Synthetic biology pathway engineering is literally compiling a desired chemical transformation into the minimum-depth enzyme circuit executable on the host organism's metabolic hardware, and the same depth-vs-fidelity tradeoffs apply.

ranked_frameworks.name: PageRank Ventilation Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Indoor air quality in complex buildings can be modeled by applying PageRank to the room-connectivity graph where directed edges represent airflow paths weighted by volumetric flow rate. Rooms with high PageRank scores accumulate pollutants because they are downstream recipients of air from many upstream zones, analogous to web pages receiving many inbound links. The damping factor maps to air exchange rate with the exterior. HVAC optimization should target not the most polluted rooms but the highest-PageRank rooms, because interventions there propagate benefits through the network's stationary distribution.

ranked_frameworks.name: Autocatalytic Set Theory of Startup Ecosystems
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Startup ecosystems achieve self-sustaining growth only when they form an autocatalytic set: each type of entity (founders, investors, universities, acquirers, talent pools, service providers) catalyzes the production of at least one other type, and the set is collectively self-sustaining under catalytic closure. Silicon Valley is a mature autocatalytic set where every species catalyzes at least one other. Failed ecosystem-building efforts (government tech parks, artificial innovation districts) fail because they inject individual molecular species without achieving catalytic closure — they provide funding and lab space but lack the acquirer-to-serial-founder recycling catalyst or the university-to-spinoff reaction. The minimum viable ecosystem can be predicted by finding the smallest autocatalytically closed subset of the full reaction network.

ranked_frameworks.name: Locard Exchange Theory of Pedagogy
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Every pedagogical contact leaves a bidirectional trace: the teacher deposits conceptual fragments in the student and simultaneously absorbs the student's naive models, misconceptions, and fresh perspectives. Teaching therefore contaminates the expert's knowledge structure with the novice's framing over time, explaining both why veteran teachers develop superior intuition for student confusion and why they progressively lose access to their own original learning trajectory. The forensic implication: the quality of any educational encounter can be assessed by examining the traces left in both directions, not just the student's test scores.

ranked_frameworks.name: Harmonic Preferential Attachment
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: In the evolution of musical styles, certain chord progressions and melodic motifs acquire disproportionate prevalence not because they are objectively superior but because they benefit from preferential attachment: the more frequently a progression appears in the canon, the more likely composers and listeners are to reference and reinforce it. This produces a power-law distribution of harmonic vocabulary usage, with a small number of 'hub' progressions (I-IV-V-I, ii-V-I) dominating, while the vast majority of possible progressions remain unexplored. Genre revolutions occur when a critical mass of musicians deliberately route new edges to low-degree nodes.

ranked_frameworks.name: Argumentative Nucleation
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Rhetorical movements crystallize through a nucleation process: scattered dissenting claims circulate as subcritical clusters in public discourse, unable to sustain themselves. When a cluster exceeds a critical mass — enough mutually reinforcing arguments with sufficient internal coherence — it overcomes the activation energy barrier of established consensus and rapidly grows into a crystalline ideological formation. Failed movements are subcritical nuclei that dissolved back into noise. This explains why identical ideas succeed at some historical moments and fail at others: the supersaturation of public frustration determines whether the nucleus can grow.

ranked_frameworks.name: Lotka-Volterra Dynamics of Attention and Content
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Online content ecosystems exhibit predator-prey oscillations where content creators (prey) and audience attention (predator) are locked in coupled dynamics. When creators are abundant and attention scarce, only the fittest content survives; the resulting high-quality pool attracts attention growth, which then enables lower-quality content to survive, diluting the pool until attention crashes. Platform health is determined by the oscillation amplitude: shallow cycles produce stable ecosystems while deep cycles produce boom-bust virality. Algorithmic recommendation acts as an environmental parameter that tunes the coupling strength between the two populations.

ranked_frameworks.name: Extinction Learning Markets
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Exposure therapy's mechanism — where repeated presentation of a feared stimulus without aversive outcome gradually overwrites the conditioned fear response through inhibitory learning — explains how financial markets recover from crash-induced risk aversion. Post-crash, market participants exhibit conditioned fear responses to stimuli associated with the crash (rising leverage ratios, yield curve inversions). Recovery occurs through 'market exposure therapy': repeated encounters with these stimuli without accompanying losses gradually build inhibitory associations. The context-specificity of extinction learning explains why recovery is fragile and fear can return when market conditions shift to resemble the original crash context.

ranked_frameworks.name: Trophic Cascade Theory of Market Regulation
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Financial regulators function as apex predators in market ecosystems: their presence suppresses predatory intermediaries (mesopredators), which in turn allows small investors (primary producers) to flourish. Regulatory removal triggers a mesopredator release — proliferation of extractive financial actors that devastate retail participation. The effect propagates across at least three trophic levels, meaning that the full impact of deregulation is not visible at the level where the regulator operated but one or two levels below.

ranked_frameworks.name: Graph Coloring Crop Rotation Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Optimal crop rotation on a spatially heterogeneous farm can be solved as a graph coloring problem where each field-season pair is a vertex, adjacency encodes spatial proximity and temporal succession, and colors represent crop families. The chromatic number of this graph gives the minimum number of crop families needed to satisfy all pest-break and nutrient-cycling constraints simultaneously. Fields sharing irrigation or drainage edges impose additional coloring constraints that standard rotation calendars miss, and the four-color theorem guarantees that planar field arrangements always admit solutions with at most four crop families.

ranked_frameworks.name: Morphogen Gradient Theory of Urban Gentrification
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Gentrification propagates through urban space as a morphogen gradient: a source neighborhood (the artsy district, the tech campus) emits a diffusing signal of rising property values and cultural capital, and surrounding neighborhoods interpret threshold concentrations as fate instructions — below one threshold they remain unchanged, above another they fully differentiate into luxury residential, and in the intermediate zone they adopt a mixed transitional phenotype. Displacement of existing populations is the developmental apoptosis of this urban morphogenesis.

ranked_frameworks.name: Erratic Displacement Theory of Diasporic Identity
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Diasporic communities are cultural erratics: geological foreigners deposited by historical glaciation events into landscapes where they are compositionally alien. Like glacial erratics, diasporic cultures develop a dual foreignness, becoming too transformed for their origin and too distinct for their location, making them permanent epistemic anomalies that reveal the movement of historical forces otherwise invisible.

ranked_frameworks.name: Prosodic Boundary Encoding in Architectural Wayfinding
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Speakers encode syntactic structure through prosodic boundaries -- pitch resets, pauses, and syllable lengthening that chunk continuous speech into parseable units. Buildings can encode circulation hierarchy through analogous 'architectural prosody': ceiling height changes, material transitions, and spatial compressions at corridor junctions serve as boundary markers that chunk continuous movement paths into cognitively parseable segments. Buildings designed with explicit prosodic boundary markers at syntactic joints (where circulation hierarchy changes) show measurably better wayfinding performance than buildings that rely solely on signage, because users parse the spatial structure using the same boundary-detection mechanisms they use for language.

ranked_frameworks.name: Network Hormesis
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Small, controlled disruptions to network topology — random node removals, temporary link failures — can paradoxically strengthen a network's long-term robustness by triggering adaptive rewiring that eliminates single points of failure. Below a critical disruption dose, the network reorganizes into a more resilient configuration with greater path redundancy; above it, the network fragments. This is hormesis applied to network architecture: moderate stress improves fitness, excessive stress destroys it. The framework predicts an optimal disruption schedule for infrastructure and organizational networks.

ranked_frameworks.name: Acoustic Impedance Theory of Cross-Cultural Communication
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Information transfer between cultural communities fails not primarily because of content differences but because of impedance mismatch — the structural properties of how each culture packages and processes information (speed, density, emotional loading, implicit vs explicit coding) differ enough to cause most communicative energy to reflect rather than transmit at the interface. Effective cross-cultural mediators function as impedance-matching transformers: they do not translate content but restructure the wave characteristics of the message to match the receiving medium. Maximum information transfer occurs when the mediator's impedance is the geometric mean of the two cultures' impedances.

ranked_frameworks.name: Liturgical Load Paths
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Structural load path analysis — where loads follow the stiffest continuous path to ground — explains how doctrinal authority propagates through religious institutional hierarchies. Theological claims (loads) are transmitted through the 'stiffest' available path: the most institutionally rigid and credentialed chain of authorities. When a link in this chain fails (a bishop defects, a seminary closes), the load redistributes to adjacent paths, sometimes overloading them. Schisms occur when no continuous load path to the foundational authority (ground) remains, and the structure must find a new foundation or collapse. Reform movements function as structural retrofits that create redundant load paths.

ranked_frameworks.name: Tobler's Law of Emotional Proximity
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Tobler's First Law of Geography — 'everything is related to everything else, but near things are more related than distant things' — when transplanted from spatial attributes to psychological space, produces a quantitative theory of emotional contagion and social influence decay. Emotional states exhibit spatial autocorrelation in 'psychosocial space' (a metric space defined by interaction frequency, relational intimacy, and identity overlap). Mood, motivation, and belief intensity follow distance-decay functions in this space, with Moran's I measuring the clustering coefficient of emotional states within social networks. This predicts that interventions targeting emotionally central individuals (low average psychosocial distance to others) will produce maximum population-level mood shift — a principle for designing mental health interventions at community scale.

ranked_frameworks.name: Autocatalytic Set Theory of Legal Precedent
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Stable legal doctrines function as autocatalytic sets: each ruling catalyzes the viability of other rulings in the network, and the set is collectively self-sustaining even though no single precedent can justify itself in isolation. Doctrinal collapse occurs not when one precedent is overturned but when enough are removed to break catalytic closure — the remaining rulings can no longer mutually sustain each other. This explains why overturning a seemingly minor case can trigger doctrinal cascade failure.

ranked_frameworks.name: Gene Regulatory Network Model of Belief System Dynamics
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: An individual's belief system is a regulatory network where beliefs activate and suppress each other like genes in a regulatory circuit. Stable worldviews correspond to network attractors — self-reinforcing patterns of belief co-expression. Conversion experiences are transitions between attractor basins, requiring perturbation strong enough to push the system past a separatrix. The number of stable worldviews available to a culture is determined by network topology, not by the number of individual beliefs, just as the number of cell types is determined by the gene regulatory network, not the gene count.

ranked_frameworks.name: Galvanic Corrosion Theory of Ocean Acidification Triage
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: In multi-species calcifying communities under ocean acidification, the most vulnerable species is sacrificially dissolved to buffer local carbonate chemistry, protecting adjacent species with higher dissolution resistance -- exactly as the anodic metal in a galvanic couple corrodes preferentially to protect the cathodic partner. The 'electrolyte' is the shared seawater; the 'potential difference' is the differential between species' aragonite saturation thresholds. Community-level calcification persistence depends not on individual species tolerance but on the galvanic hierarchy of dissolution potentials, and losing the sacrificial species removes protection for the next-most-vulnerable, triggering a corrosion cascade.

ranked_frameworks.name: Shannon Entropy Weathering Index
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: The degree of rock weathering at a geological surface can be quantified as the Shannon entropy of its mineral grain size distribution. Fresh unweathered rock has low entropy (dominated by few crystal size classes), while deeply weathered rock approaches maximum entropy as diverse chemical and mechanical processes fragment grains into a uniform distribution across size classes. This entropy measure provides a single scalar index that integrates all weathering processes without requiring identification of individual mechanisms, and its rate of increase with depth follows channel-capacity-like bounds set by local climate and lithology.

ranked_frameworks.name: Bullwhip Effect in Liturgical Renewal
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Liturgical reform in hierarchical religious institutions amplifies variance at each echelon of implementation, identical to the bullwhip effect in supply chains. A modest doctrinal signal from the central authority (e.g., Vatican II's 'noble simplicity') is interpreted with increasing variance by each downstream node — national bishops' conferences, diocesan liturgical offices, parish priests, volunteer music directors — until the on-the-ground implementation bears little resemblance to the original signal. Each echelon adds its own forecast error (what do they really mean?) and safety stock (better go further than intended to show compliance). The result is liturgical inventory oscillation: overcorrection followed by backlash, with period and amplitude increasing at greater distance from the source signal.

ranked_frameworks.name: Magma Chamber Theory of Revolutionary Movements
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Social revolutions follow magma chamber dynamics: grievances accumulate as slow recharge into a crustal reservoir of collective discontent, crystallization of moderate reforms reduces the volatile fraction, and eruption occurs only when the internal pressure of unaddressed demands exceeds the lithostatic threshold set by state repressive capacity. The eruption style — effusive reform vs. explosive revolution — is determined by the volatile content (ideological radicalism) and the viscosity (social cohesion) of the accumulated discontent, not by any single triggering event.

ranked_frameworks.name: Stigmergic Network Convergence Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Optimal transport networks can emerge without centralized planning through a reinforce-or-decay feedback loop operating on flow volume, and such networks converge toward mathematically similar topologies regardless of whether the substrate is biological or infrastructural. The key requirement is that the medium itself carries the signal — making the network its own optimization algorithm.

ranked_frameworks.name: Quorum Pricing
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Markets exhibit quorum-sensing-like behavior: individual firms do not adjust prices based solely on their own cost signals but instead respond to the aggregate density of competitor price changes in their local market. Below a threshold density of price-movers, firms hold steady (analogous to bacterial quiescence); above it, a coordinated repricing cascade sweeps the sector. This reframes price stickiness not as irrational friction but as a rational wait-for-quorum strategy that conserves adjustment costs until collective action is likely.

ranked_frameworks.name: Dislocation Theory of Institutional Reform
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Institutions deform under political stress not through uniform yielding but through the propagation of discrete reform dislocations — minimal structural rearrangements that move through the institutional lattice one bond at a time. Like crystal dislocations, each reform step requires far less energy than would be needed to shear the entire institution simultaneously, explaining how massive institutional changes accumulate from individually minor procedural adjustments. Reform hardening occurs as dislocations pile up at institutional grain boundaries (departmental borders, jurisdictional lines), and sustained reform requires periodic 'annealing' — deliberate structural relaxation that clears dislocation tangles.

ranked_frameworks.name: Quasispecies Jurisprudence
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Eigen's quasispecies theory — where selection in high-mutation-rate systems acts on a cloud of mutant variants rather than individual sequences — reframes how legal doctrines evolve under common law. A legal doctrine is not a single rule but a quasispecies: a cloud of varying judicial interpretations centered on a consensus reading. Selection pressure (appeals court rulings, legislative response) acts on the distribution of interpretations, not on any single holding. The 'error threshold' concept explains doctrinal collapse: when lower courts vary interpretations beyond a critical rate, the doctrine loses coherent identity and must be replaced by statute or landmark ruling — a legal analogue to the error catastrophe.

ranked_frameworks.name: HPA Axis Model of Economic Stress Response
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: National economies respond to shocks through a three-tier negative-feedback loop structurally isomorphic to the HPA hormonal axis. The initial alarm (market panic) triggers a hypothalamic signal (central bank communication), which activates a pituitary relay (interbank lending rate adjustment), which drives a cortisol-like effector (broad monetary policy). Negative feedback — economic stabilization suppressing further intervention — normally restores baseline. Chronic economic stress, however, downregulates receptors (market participants become desensitized to policy signals), producing a persistent elevated-intervention state analogous to adrenal fatigue.

ranked_frameworks.name: Zone Refining of Rhythmic Entrainment
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Musical ensemble rehearsal achieves timing purity through a process analogous to zone refining in metallurgy. A narrow 'molten zone' of focused attention sweeps through a piece passage by passage, and timing impurities (micro-timing deviations) with segregation coefficients less than one are swept toward the boundaries of rehearsed sections, concentrating at transitions. Multiple rehearsal passes progressively purify the interior timing while impurities accumulate at section boundaries, which is why transitions between sections remain the last timing problems solved and require disproportionate rehearsal investment.

ranked_frameworks.name: Structural Holes in Soil Pore Networks
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Soil fertility hotspots correspond to structural holes in the soil pore network: locations where otherwise disconnected pore clusters are bridged by a single macropore or root channel. These broker pores control the flow of water, dissolved nutrients, and microbial colonizers between isolated microsites, analogous to Burt's network brokers gaining information advantages by bridging disconnected social clusters. Soil organisms that colonize structural-hole pores gain disproportionate access to diverse nutrient streams and become keystone species for local biogeochemical cycling. Tillage practices that destroy macropore bridges eliminate these structural holes and homogenize the pore network, paradoxically reducing nutrient cycling efficiency despite increasing total porosity.

ranked_frameworks.name: Stratigraphic Superposition of Software Architecture
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Legacy software systems accumulate architectural layers through a process identical to geological deposition: each era's dominant paradigm (structured programming, object-orientation, microservices, serverless) deposits a stratum of code, and the principle of superposition holds — deeper layers are older unless disturbed by refactoring events analogous to tectonic intrusions. The archaeological method of Harris matrix analysis can be applied to codebases: by mapping cutting relations (where newer code interrupts older layers), interfacial units (API boundaries), and depositional contexts (git blame epochs), engineers can reconstruct a site chronology of the system that reveals its true load-bearing strata versus superficial recent deposits, guiding modernization efforts toward the actual foundation layers.

ranked_frameworks.name: Demographic Transition Model of Language Death
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Endangered languages die through a demographic transition: in Stage 1 (pre-contact), high rates of both language innovation (neologism, dialectal drift) and language loss (forgetting of archaic forms) maintain a stable linguistic population. In Stage 2 (early contact with a dominant language), loss of archaic forms accelerates while innovation continues, producing a net vocabulary expansion through borrowing. In Stage 3 (advanced shift), innovation collapses as young speakers switch to the dominant language, but loss accelerates — the language population crashes. In Stage 4 (post-shift), a tiny stable remnant of ceremonial or heritage speakers persists at low equilibrium. Intervention is most effective at the Stage 2-3 boundary, analogous to family planning programs being most effective during the demographic transition's inflection point. Once Stage 3 is well underway, revitalization requires the linguistic equivalent of pro-natalist policy: artificial incentive structures that reverse the natural adoption of the dominant language.

ranked_frameworks.name: Hormetic Theory of Democratic Stress
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Democracies exhibit a hormetic dose-response to political crisis: low-dose stressors (contested elections, moderate protest, judicial challenges) trigger adaptive institutional responses that strengthen democratic resilience, but high-dose stressors (coups, civil war, hyperinflation) overwhelm adaptive capacity and cause democratic collapse. The narrow dose window explains the paradox that democracies which have never been tested are more fragile than those that have survived moderate crises, and why deliberately shielding democracies from all stress produces institutional atrophy indistinguishable from the damage caused by excessive stress.

ranked_frameworks.name: Hostile Medium Preservation Paradox
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Cultural archives function as institutional peat bogs: they preserve cultural artifacts by creating conditions hostile to the living culture that produced them — decontextualizing, dehydrating practice into artifact, and suspending organic cultural processes in institutional anoxia. The paradox is structural: the very conditions that enable preservation are antagonistic to the living use, mutation, and communal ownership that defined the culture being preserved.

ranked_frameworks.name: Syntactic Prediction Gradient in Epidemiological Forecasting
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: The neurolinguistic mechanism by which the brain maintains competing syntactic parse trees and prunes them via surprisal-driven reanalysis maps onto how epidemiological models should handle competing transmission hypotheses during an emerging outbreak. Just as a garden-path sentence forces costly reanalysis when initial syntactic predictions fail, an epidemic surveillance system that commits too early to one transmission model (airborne vs. fomite vs. vector) incurs high 'reanalysis cost' when anomalous data forces model revision. Optimal outbreak response maintains multiple competing parse trees of transmission dynamics, pruning via Bayesian surprisal from incoming case data.

ranked_frameworks.name: Warp Tension Equilibrium Jurisprudence
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Legal doctrines within a jurisdiction behave like warp yarns under tension on a loom: each doctrine is held taut by constitutional anchors at both ends, and new case law (the weft) interlaces through them, redistributing stress across the entire fabric. Doctrinal coherence emerges not from any single thread's strength but from the collective crimp geometry — the way each doctrine bends around its neighbors. When a court overrules a precedent, it is severing a warp thread, and the resulting tension redistribution can propagate structural distortion far from the original cut, explaining why narrow rulings sometimes cascade into wholesale doctrinal realignment.

ranked_frameworks.name: Choreographic Stigmergy
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Improvisational dance ensembles coordinate without verbal communication through stigmergic traces left in shared space-time: a dancer's movement through a stage region leaves a spatial-kinesthetic residue (wake turbulence, visual afterimage, rhythmic pattern) that subsequent dancers read and respond to. The stage floor becomes a medium of indirect communication analogous to pheromone trails. This framework explains how contact improvisation groups achieve coherent emergent structures without choreographic authority and predicts that ensemble coherence scales with the persistence time of these movement traces.

ranked_frameworks.name: Quasispecies Theory of Startup Ecosystems
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Startup ecosystems function as quasispecies: the unit of competitive selection is not the individual company but the mutant cloud of related ventures exploring adjacent business models simultaneously. An ecosystem's fitness is determined by the mean viability of this swarm, not by any single firm's performance. There exists an 'error threshold' in startup pivoting: when the rate of business model mutation exceeds the inverse of the ecosystem's complexity, the swarm loses coherence and dissolves into random noise. Successful venture capital implicitly manages mutation rate to stay just below this threshold, maximizing exploration without crossing into incoherence.

ranked_frameworks.name: Magma Chamber Theory of Revolutionary Pressure
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Social revolutions are governed by pressure-vessel dynamics identical to volcanic eruptions: grievances accumulate as recharge flux into a crustal reservoir of discontent, while institutional safety valves (free press, elections, protests) act as volatile exsolution vents. Revolution triggers when accumulated social pressure exceeds the lithostatic threshold of institutional containment — and the eruption style (reformist effusion vs violent explosion) is determined by the viscosity of social structure, which is itself a function of inequality crystallization. The most dangerous configuration is a society with high recharge rate, sealed vents, and high structural viscosity: it guarantees explosive rather than effusive discharge.

ranked_frameworks.name: Choreographic Multiplier Effect
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: The Keynesian multiplier — where a single injection of spending propagates through an economy via marginal propensity to consume — maps onto choreographic ensemble dynamics. A single dancer's movement phrase, when injected into an ensemble operating with high 'propensity to respond' (trained mimetic sensitivity), propagates and amplifies through successive dancer-to-dancer transmissions, producing a total kinetic output that exceeds the initial movement by a factor determined by the ensemble's responsiveness coefficient. Choreographers can tune this multiplier by adjusting rehearsal regimes that govern how readily each dancer absorbs and retransmits movement impulses.

ranked_frameworks.name: Auxin Gradient Economics
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Plant hormonal signaling via auxin — where concentration gradients across tissues direct growth through polar transport and threshold-dependent cell responses — provides a model for how investment capital shapes regional economic development. Capital, like auxin, flows directionally through institutional channels (banks, venture funds, government programs) and accumulates in specific zones. Above certain concentration thresholds, capital triggers 'economic cell elongation' (business formation, infrastructure growth). The 'gravitropic response' equivalent is how capital flows toward existing economic mass, while policy interventions function as exogenous auxin application — redirecting growth toward underserved regions by artificially establishing concentration gradients.

ranked_frameworks.name: Thermohaline Stratification of Social Trust
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Social trust in large institutions forms density-driven layers analogous to ocean thermohaline circulation. Surface trust (public opinion) is volatile and responsive to media temperature, while deep trust (institutional legitimacy) is cold, saline, and slow-moving. Trust crises occur when surface disruptions penetrate deep enough to destabilize the density gradient, triggering a turnover event where previously buried distrust rapidly surfaces. Restratification after such events takes decades because deep trust, like deep water, forms only under sustained conditions of low turbulence.

ranked_frameworks.name: Topological Error Correction Choreography
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Ensemble choreography can be made robust to individual dancer errors by encoding movement phrases across redundant dancer subgroups, analogous to how topological quantum error correction distributes a logical qubit across many physical qubits. When a dancer deviates, the surrounding ensemble's spatial and temporal relationships act as syndrome measurements that allow the audience to perceive the intended pattern despite local faults. The choreographic 'logical phrase' survives as long as the fraction of erring dancers stays below a fault-tolerance threshold determined by ensemble density and spatial redundancy.

ranked_frameworks.name: Nash Equilibrium Soil Horizon Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Soil horizons stabilize at depths where no single weathering process (chemical dissolution, frost heaving, root penetration, bioturbation) can unilaterally shift the horizon boundary without triggering compensating responses from the others. Each horizon represents a Nash equilibrium of competing pedogenic agents: clay illuviation, organic matter accumulation, and mineral weathering reach mutual best-response configurations under the local climate regime. Disturbing one process (e.g., deforestation removing root penetration) destabilizes the equilibrium and causes horizon boundaries to migrate until a new Nash state is reached, often on decadal timescales.

ranked_frameworks.name: Acoustic Impedance Theory of Pedagogical Transfer
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Learning transfer between domains fails or succeeds based on impedance matching: each knowledge domain has a characteristic impedance determined by its abstraction density (analogous to medium density) and its procedural fluency demands (analogous to wave velocity). When a learner moves from one domain to another, the fraction of understanding that transmits across the boundary is governed by the impedance mismatch between the two domains. Maximum transfer occurs when impedances are matched — explaining why mathematics transfers well to physics (similar impedance) but poorly to literary criticism (massive mismatch). Effective pedagogy inserts impedance-matching layers (bridging analogies, worked examples in the target domain's idiom) that function like acoustic coupling gels, maximizing the transmitted energy fraction of prior knowledge.

ranked_frameworks.name: Quasispecies Theory of Legal Doctrine
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Legal doctrines persist not as single canonical interpretations but as quasispecies clouds — distributions of slightly variant readings maintained by the high mutation rate of case-by-case application. Selection acts on the cloud's mean fitness, not on any individual interpretation, explaining why doctrines survive despite internally contradictory precedents. An error threshold exists: when the rate of interpretive mutation (driven by judicial creativity, novel fact patterns, or ideological diversity on courts) exceeds the inverse of the doctrine's logical complexity, the doctrine undergoes an error catastrophe and fragments into incoherent sub-doctrines.

ranked_frameworks.name: Commitment Erosion Dynamics in Fermentation Process Control
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: In game theory, commitment devices erode as players test the rigidity of binding agreements over successive rounds. In industrial fermentation, process control setpoints (temperature, pH, dissolved oxygen) function as 'commitments' that the microbial community tests over successive generations. Rigid setpoints attract stable microbial cooperation but cannot adapt to drift in culture composition; flexible setpoints enable adaptation but invite opportunistic strain takeover. The commitment erosion model predicts an optimal setpoint adjustment frequency for each fermentation stage -- rigid during exponential growth (when cooperation is critical) and increasingly flexible during stationary phase (when adaptation matters more).

ranked_frameworks.name: Zero-Knowledge Courtroom Protocol
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Certain legal procedures — particularly trade-secret litigation and classified-evidence hearings — already approximate zero-knowledge proof systems: one party must convince the court of a fact's truth without revealing the fact itself. Formalizing this analogy suggests that the soundness guarantees of ZK protocols could be imported to design legal discovery procedures where the verifier (judge) gains mathematical confidence in a claim's validity while the witness (sensitive information) is never exposed. The key constraint is that legal 'verifiers' operate with bounded rationality rather than polynomial-time computation, requiring protocol designs that account for cognitive rather than computational limits.

ranked_frameworks.name: Rhetorical Predator-Prey Cycles
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Public discourse exhibits Lotka-Volterra oscillations between persuasive strategies and audience defenses. When a novel rhetorical technique (e.g., data storytelling, manufactured authenticity) proliferates, audience skepticism co-evolves to neutralize it, causing the technique's effectiveness to crash. This creates ecological space for new persuasive mutations. The coupled oscillation between rhetorical innovation and audience resistance explains why persuasion techniques cycle through effectiveness peaks and troughs on roughly generational timescales, and why nostalgia for 'simpler' argumentation periodically resurfaces.

ranked_frameworks.name: Circadian Oscillator Model of Creative Output
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Sustained creative productivity in individuals and teams follows a transcription-translation feedback loop architecture: a period of uninhibited idea generation (transcription) produces raw material that accumulates until it triggers a negative feedback phase of critical editing and refinement (translation/degradation), which suppresses further generation until the critical product decays, re-enabling the generative phase. The period of this oscillation is individually tuned and disrupting it — by demanding constant generation or constant refinement — destroys output quality. Optimal creative management requires identifying and respecting each creator's endogenous oscillation period rather than imposing external schedules.

ranked_frameworks.name: Liminal Code Review
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Turner's concept of liminality — where ritual participants pass through a threshold state that dissolves old social structure and enables transformation via communitas — describes the dynamics of effective code review processes. The pull request creates a liminal space: code is separated from the author's private branch (separation phase), enters a state where it belongs to neither the author nor the codebase (liminal margin), and is reincorporated into main only after transformation. The communitas of reviewers temporarily suspends normal hierarchy (junior devs can block senior devs' code), and the symbolic inversion of expertise (the author becomes the explained-to) is what makes review transformative rather than merely gatekeeping.

ranked_frameworks.name: Soil Horizon Theory of Institutional Memory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Institutional knowledge stratifies into horizons analogous to soil formation. The O-horizon (surface organic) is active tacit knowledge held by current employees. The A-horizon is documented procedures mixed with organizational culture. The B-horizon contains accumulated but compacted precedents accessible only through deliberate excavation. The C-horizon is the founding bedrock of charter assumptions. Organizational health depends on active bioturbation — vertical mixing by boundary-spanning employees — without which the topsoil of active knowledge erodes and the institution operates on increasingly irrelevant deep layers.

ranked_frameworks.name: Grain Boundary Strengthening of Ensemble Dance
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: The visual and kinetic strength of ensemble choreography increases as the number of distinct dancer subgroups (analogous to crystallographic grains) increases, following a Hall-Petch-like relationship. Each boundary between subgroups performing different phrases creates a visual 'dislocation barrier' that prevents the audience's attention from sliding uniformly across the stage. Finer-grained ensemble subdivision produces stronger visual impact up to a critical point, below which the groups become too small to read as distinct units and the effect inverts (inverse Hall-Petch), collapsing into visual noise.

ranked_frameworks.name: Queueing Theory of Atmospheric Precipitation
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Cloud droplet coalescence and precipitation onset can be modeled as an M/M/1 queueing system where water vapor molecules are arriving jobs, condensation nuclei are servers, and precipitation occurs when the queue length (supersaturation) exceeds a critical threshold analogous to queue instability when arrival rate exceeds service rate. The average wait time before precipitation maps to the delay between moisture advection and rainfall onset, and Little's Law predicts the steady-state atmospheric water content from the arrival rate and mean residence time. This framework explains why light drizzle (stable queue near capacity) and convective downpours (queue blowup) are qualitatively different regimes separated by a sharp utilization threshold.

ranked_frameworks.name: Dose-Response Homiletics
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: The persuasive effect of a sermon or public moral exhortation follows a sigmoidal dose-response curve: below a threshold intensity, the message produces no behavioral change (sub-therapeutic dose); within a therapeutic window, increasing emotional and logical intensity yields proportional moral response; but beyond an upper threshold, the audience develops receptor downregulation — defensive psychological numbness that renders further exhortation not just ineffective but actively counterproductive, creating moral fatigue. The EC50 (half-maximal effective concentration) of a moral message is set by the audience's prior exposure history, explaining why prophetic traditions that alternate silence and speech outperform those that maintain constant exhortation. Preaching schedules should be designed like dosing regimens, with attention to clearance rates of moral urgency.

ranked_frameworks.name: Red Queen Hypothesis of Musical Genre Evolution
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Musical genres and their audiences are locked in a Red Queen co-evolutionary arms race: artists must continuously mutate their sound to evade listener habituation (the parasite that drains attention), while listeners evolve increasingly sophisticated pattern-recognition that renders yesterday's novelty into today's cliche. Genres that reproduce sexually (collaborative cross-pollination between artists) maintain higher variation and resist habituation longer than genres dominated by a single auteur's clonal output. The rare-genotype advantage explains why obscure subgenres experience sudden popularity surges — audiences whose habituation-resistance has been eroded by mainstream exposure are uniquely susceptible to unfamiliar sonic genotypes.

ranked_frameworks.name: Dislocation Theory of Social Mobility
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Social stratification resists rearrangement not because individuals lack merit but because social structures, like crystal lattices, deform through the motion of line defects rather than through bulk displacement. Social mobility occurs primarily at dislocations — localized disruptions in the class lattice (new industries, immigrant enclaves, institutional scholarships) — that glide along slip planes under applied stress (economic growth, policy pressure). The Peierls barrier analog explains why mobility requires a threshold level of societal stress before any movement occurs, and why some social structures (high-symmetry meritocracies) permit easier dislocation glide than others (low-symmetry caste systems).

ranked_frameworks.name: Critical Differentiation Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Cell fate commitment operates as a bona fide phase transition in gene-expression state space, where signaling gradients tune cells to a critical point at which free-energy minimization selects one stable attractor from a symmetry-broken manifold.

ranked_frameworks.name: MycelialRank: Adaptive Nutrient-Flow Page Ranking
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Web page authority is better modeled as an adaptive resource-routing network where link pathways strengthen or atrophy based on user-traffic gradients, rather than as a static stationary distribution over a fixed graph.

ranked_frameworks.name: Swarm Multiplier Economics
